name: Nitrofurantoin
description: A bacteriostatic or bactericidal agent depending on the concentration and susceptibility of the infecting organism. Nitrofurantoin is active against some gram positive organisms such as S. aureus, S. epidermidis, S. saprophyticus, Enterococcus faecalis, S. agalactiae, group D streptococci, viridians streptococci and Corynebacterium. Its spectrum of activity against gram negative organisms includes E. coli, Enterobacter, Neisseria, Salmonella and Shigella. It may be used as an alternative to trimethoprim/sulfamethoxazole for treating urinary tract infections though it may be less effective at eradicating vaginal bacteria. May also be used in females as prophylaxis against recurrent cystitis related to coitus. Nitrofurantoin is highly stable to the development of bacterial resistance, a property thought to be due to its multiplicity of mechanisms of action.
indication: May be used as an alternative in the treatment of urinary tract infections. May be used by females pericoitally for prophylaxis against recurrent cystitis related to coitus.
toxicity: Acute toxicity may cause vomiting. Adverse effects include nausea and urine discolouration. Rare hepatotoxic and hypersensitivity reactions have occurred. Hemolytic anemia is a risk in patients with G6PD deficiency. Ascending polyneuropathy may occur with prolonged therapy or in patients with low creatinine clearance.
mechanismOfAction: Nitrofurantoin is activated by bacterial flavoproteins (nitrofuran reductase) to active reduced reactive intermediates that are thought to modulate and damage ribosomal proteins or other macromolecules, especially DNA, causing inhibition of DNA, RNA, protein, and cell wall synthesis. The overall effect is inhibition of bacterial growth or cell death.
pharmacodynamics: Nitrofurantoin exhibits bacteriostatic or bactericidal effects by inhibiting the synthesis of DNA, RNA, protein and cell wall synthesis.

interactions.name: Magnesium Trisilicate
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Nitrofurantoin can be decreased when it is combined with Magnesium Trisilicate.

interactions.name: Norfloxacin
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Norfloxacin can be decreased when used in combination with Nitrofurantoin.

interactions.name: Spironolactone
interactions.description: Nitrofurantoin may increase the hyperkalemic activities of Spironolactone.

interactions.name: Vemurafenib
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Vemurafenib can be decreased when it is combined with Nitrofurantoin.

interactions.name: Dapsone
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Dapsone is combined with Nitrofurantoin.

interactions.name: Nitric Oxide
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Nitric Oxide is combined with Nitrofurantoin.

interactions.name: Prilocaine
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Nitrofurantoin is combined with Prilocaine.

interactions.name: Sodium Nitrite
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Nitrofurantoin is combined with Sodium Nitrite.

interactions.name: Rolapitant
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Nitrofurantoin can be increased when it is combined with Rolapitant.

interactions.name: Eplerenone
interactions.description: Nitrofurantoin may increase the hyperkalemic activities of Eplerenone.

interactions.name: Probenecid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Nitrofurantoin can be increased when it is combined with Probenecid.

interactions.name: Eltrombopag
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Nitrofurantoin can be increased when it is combined with Eltrombopag.

interactions.name: Teriflunomide
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Nitrofurantoin can be increased when it is combined with Teriflunomide.
